title: Level 361
wikitext:
__NOTOC__
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 361: "Creaking Stairway"}} {{Image Banner|image=Stair1.jpg}} {{Alt Class|no=361|ext=3|env=2|ent=2}} Level 361 is the 362nd level of the Backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=Stair1.jpg|480x480px|caption=The staircase illuminated by the iPhone 11 flashlight.}} ==Description== '''Level 361''', otherwise known as “Creaking Stairway”, is an exceedingly long, wooden staircase that creaks and groans as it is traversed. The loudness of the creaking increases gradually as the wanderer descends, eventually reaching hearing-damaging decibels. These sounds often invoke feelings of anxiety and dread, accompanied by dizziness and headaches. These effects are amplified by Level 361's lack of light and the powerful musk of mildew. Wanderers estimate the staircase has around 4000 steps, and that reaching the bottom usually takes an hour if walking at a steady pace. The stairway is narrow, and the wallpaper is tattered and ruined, sometimes even completely deteriorated. The level is also under the effect of the [[Phenomenon 15|Alone phenomenon]]. Occasionally, protruding from the walls or ceiling are green-and-purple fleshy organs. The origin of these vine-like limbs is unknown, but they do appear vascular, as though from some sort of entity or animal. Some of these limbs have sharp spikes that appear like leaves. These leaf-like spikes are sharp enough to puncture or slice skin. When in contact, these organs release a toxic chemical that is an irritant to the lungs and eyes. The composition of this chemical remains untested, so it is advised to avoid touching these vine-like limbs altogether. After an undetermined amount of time, as a wanderer descends in Level 361, they may rarely find a vibrating smartphone that will magically appear in their pocket with a single email notification. The iPhone is fully functional and charged. The email will appear to be an urgent message from a distant family member, with the message providing instructions on how to enter a secret room in this level. The connection between this anomaly and the level is yet to be looked into. The following is an example of what might appear in the email:
{| class="wikitable" style="border:1px solid #dcdcdc; width: 1000px; background-color: #f9f9f9;" |+ | style="background-color: #D3D3D3; border-bottom: 1px solid #000000; padding: 10px; color: black;" | '''From:''' Uncle_Gary@YooMail.com |- | style="background-color: #D3D3D3; border-bottom: 1px solid #000000; padding: 10px; color: black;" | '''To:''' {{USERNAME}}@OGMail.com |- | style="background-color: #f4f4f4; border-bottom: 1px solid #dcdcdc; padding: 10px; color: black;" | '''Subject:''' Urgent Message |- | style="padding: 10px; color: black;" | {{USERNAME}}! I haven't seen you in ages! How have you been? It's your uncle! Well, your distant uncle on your mother's side. You still remember your mother, right? I have some information regarding the level you're on. Yeah, I know about the backrooms and Level 361. So count carefully, go up to the top of the stairs, and then go down to the 361st step. Noclip through the right wall, and you'll end up in a secret room. It's the only safe way out of the level. Good luck! Love, Your favorite Uncle |}
== == There are four notable sections in '''Level 361''':
  • The Staircase
  • The Secret Room
  • The Bottom
  • The Beyond
The top of the staircase is where all wanderers first arrive when noclipping into Level 361. It is devoid of entities and is considered to be one of the safest parts of the level. As previously stated, the stairs are wooden, and the wallpaper is torn and ruined. There are vine-like organs with sharp leaf-like spines occasionally present in the ceiling and walls. The stairs creak as they are stepped on, and the noise of that creak increases gradually as wanderers descend. The lower the wanderer traverses, the higher the chances are that they will encounter this level's native entities. A smartphone may rarely appear in your pocket as well. == ==
To find the secret room, as per instructed in the urgent email found on said smartphone, one must count and descend to the 361st step from the top of the staircase and noclip straight through the nearby wall. The room (according to various testimonies) is partially filled with crates that hold useful supplies such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] or weapons. On the back wall, facing the entry point to the room, is an arcade cabinet titled "The Backrooms." This game is an 8-bit adventure with gameplay reminiscent of Level 361. In the game, wanderers will need to descend the staircase and reach the bottom, avoiding vines and shadow entities along the way. If a wanderer makes it to the end of the game, arriving and surviving the final wave of monsters at the bottom, they will fall through the cabinet and end up in a random level. There are unlimited retries when playing the game. If a wanderer is unsuccessful during their first playthrough, they may restart and try again. Each playthrough, however, the screen will darken as though a mysterious force is swallowing the level in darkness. It is rumored that finding the secret room inside the game allows for the player to readjust the brightness and unlock a secret ending to it, but this has not been verified. Next to the cabinet is a "cheat token" that, if inserted into the machine, will roll the game credits, and the wanderer will again fall through the machine, this time ending up in [[The Game]]. Note that there are no entities ever present in the secret room. == ==
To reach the bottom of the staircase, wanderers must descend around 4000 steps, usually taking around an hour on average. The space at the bottom resembles an abandoned and rotten supply room, with shelves holding rusted tools such as hammers and wrenches. This is the only place in Level 361 that has light, as a single, naked bulb hangs from the ceiling, barely lighting the area. Behind one of the shelves is a wooden door painted red, with wanderers who grab the brass doorknob of said door, describing feelings of panic and even adrenaline. It is hypothesized that when a wanderer touches the doorknob of the red door, the level can read the heart and mind of said wanderer. Once the door is opened, whatever appears on the other side is based on the wanderer's greatest fear. For example, wanderers who have a fear of clowns and spiders will possibly find a killer clown accompanied by a swarm of spiders on the other side. These events last an undetermined amount of time, and though they are psychologically damaging, they are not physically dangerous. If a wanderer manages to endure these horrors, the door will present itself as a passage to [[Level 918]]. == ==
Hidden on the floor somewhere at the bottom of the staircase is a secret hatch. Opening the hatch reveals an impossibly bright, white space. Wanderers describe being momentarily blinded by the sudden light in contrast to the general darkness of the level. Once a wanderer's eyes adjust, they may notice shadows drifting in the blanket of light. If one closes their eyes, they might hear whispers, rumored to be lost souls. Information about the section has only ever come from photographs and recordings, as all wanderers that have entered The Beyond have never been heard from or seen again. Nothing else is known about The Beyond. == ==
==
A Cry For Help
== '''Level 361''' is not what it seems. I thought it would be another boring level that stretches on forever with nothing in sight, but no. It was something else. I arrived at '''Level 361''' with my brother, Richard, shortly after breaking free of a Smiler's grip. The level was fairly small in size, the wallpaper had been completely ripped apart as if something bulldozed through the level. Something strange was also growing out of the roof, strange vines, and I wanted to touch it, but my brother told me not to. Even though he sounded a bit harsh, I listened, as I trusted him and knew I would be safe under his guidance. We decided to go down the flight of stairs, and as we continued to go down, we realized that there was a possibility that it was endless, and that walking down would be a waste of time. Although I wanted to go back, my brother forced me to go down the stairs with him. However, I could no longer see since it was pitch dark. As we went down, we shared funny stories to lighten up our moods. Suddenly, I heard a buzzing noise coming from my right pocket. It was a phone, and I had received an urgent email. My brother had also received the same email at the same time. Despite being surprised by our newfound devices mysteriously appearing in our pockets, we opened up the email and read a message from someone claiming to be a distant relative. My "uncle", to be specific. It was instructions for getting to a 'secret room' by going to the 361st step. We were no fools, and the trap was way too obvious, so we ignored the email and closed the app. At least the phone's flashlight would come in handy, I thought. Richard and I continued to go down the stairs. And after a while, we finally reached the bottom. It was a rotten supply room, and we picked up whatever we could find. On the other side was a mysterious red door. Curiosity got the better of us, so we decided to open it. That was our biggest mistake. Chaos was unleashed as we were being chased by indescribable creatures. Richard told me to keep running, so I did without ever looking back. I scanned the room and raced to find an exit. The hatch on the floor was my way out, so I quickly entered it. I dropped down onto an invisible floor, quickly observing my surroundings. I was in a white void. Suddenly, I saw shadows, floating in the air, whispering in an unknown language. I was sure that I was hallucinating, and I knew I would not make it if I kept wasting time watching the shadows go by. So I ran. I ran carefree, ignoring everything in my path, without a worry in the world. Soon, I felt the cold breeze, and I saw the Sun shining on me. '''But where was I?'''
==Entities== There are two level-exclusive entities that can be found in '''Level 361''': ===Reflection=== Reflection is one of the first entities one may encounter throughout their journey in '''Level 361'''. It takes the form of the wanderer's shadow, with slight differences in appearance. Hair cannot be seen in the shadows, and sharp spikes may stick out of the shadow's skin. Simply noticing these slight changes to one's shadow will revert your shadow to normal, and Reflection seems to disperse. However, if the shadow goes unnoticed, the entity will strike, tearing the wanderer's skin until death. Reflection avoids light but is capable of fast movement. If one is being attacked, they should try to find a place with light, or quickly use the phone's flashlight to illuminate themself. Chances are, they will receive many injuries, but it is possible to survive using this method. It is highly advised to just pay attention to your shadow and keep an eye on any changes. '''It is unknown whether Reflection can take on the form of things other than shadows.''' ===Masked=== Masked is a rare entity that can be found on the 361st step. It resembles a floating white mask surrounded by black smoke. Once one arrives at the 361st step, they have a small chance of encountering Masked. Masked is incapable of physically attacking wanderers, but it will make an ear-piercing screech that usually lasts around a minute, capable of rupturing one's eardrums. Aside from the information above, nothing else is known about Masked. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the nature of this level and the effects of the [[Phenomenon 15|Alone phenomenon]], no colonies or outposts have been established. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Falling through a deteriorated staircase in [[Level 350]] will lead here. *Encounter a black shadow floating in [[Level 10]]. ===Exits=== *Completing the arcade game from the secret room will send you to a random level. *Beating the arcade game using the cheat chip will send you to [[The Game]]. *Running in The Beyond will lead you to an unknown location. *Face your fears from the red door at the bottom, and the door will manifest as a passage to [[Level 914]]. I like video games! I like masks! Let's go play on the stairs! {{Collapse|initial=Open Credits|final=Close Credits|body= Rewrite: [[User:TheDadams|Dadams]], [[User:NormalDudeWithaNormalLife|Normal]]
